channel.title: Linux on Scorpiosec
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on Scorpiosec

item.title: Overview of the Common Linux File System Types
item.description: Introduction Last week, we explored the organization of files in Linux, but how does the operating system automatically know where to store file “X” or file “Y”? The answer lies in the file system type—a crucial blueprint that dictates the organization and storage of data on a computer’s storage devices, such as hard drives or solid-state drives. It guides the computer to manage and keep track of files and folders.

item.title: Demystifying the Linux File System Hierarchy
item.description: Linux File Hierarchy Structure Proficiency in Linux is indispensable for cybersecurity professionals, you may have noticed that Linux is different from Windows in the way files are organized. The Filesystem Hierarchy Standard (FHS) maintained by the Linux Foundation provides a model for the directories structure. While there may be some variations across Linux distributions, they typically adhere to this standard. To enhance your Linux skills, a fundamental understanding of the file system is essential.
